summary: Shepherd-driver Phase X.2 decision policy under a panel CEO ship_with_followups return carrying 4 recommended follow-ups. Three items raise the quality bar of the PR's stated scope (missing test for the new flag, CHANGELOG entry, warning ergonomics on the new error message). One item is a wholly different theme (cross-cutting refactor of the auth resolver, sparked by a one-file CLI fix). With the skill loaded, the shepherd-driver applies assets/fold-vs-defer-rubric.md: FOLD the 3 scope-aligned items, DEFER 1 with a scope_boundary_crossed citation, increment outer_iterations, and continue (not terminal yet -- a fold-round still needs another panel pass). Without the skill, the orchestrator posts a generic advisory comment and asks the maintainer to triage, the load-bearing anti-pattern.

scenario.user_query: structured fixture: panel CEO returned ship_with_followups for PR #1490 (one-file fix that adds a --no-color flag to apm run). recommended_followups = [missing test for --no-color, CHANGELOG entry, warning wording when TERM is dumb, refactor auth resolver to share token cache with run]. shepherd-driver Phase X.2 decision required.

ground_truth.deferred_item: refactor auth resolver to share token cache with run
ground_truth.next_phase: X.3 (edit code, fold every FOLD item)

expected_phases_with_skill: Phase X.2 begins; CEO return parsed; 4 follow-ups extracted
fold-vs-defer rubric applied per item; axis = scope-creep risk relative to stated scope
items 1,2,3 marked FOLD (test for new behavior, CHANGELOG for this change, ergonomics on new surface)
item 4 marked DEFER with scope_boundary_crossed citation (auth refactor != --no-color flag)
outer_iterations incremented; loop continues to Phase X.3

rubric.description: The fold-vs-defer rubric is explicitly named as the decision authority.
Decision axis is named as scope-creep risk relative to stated scope (not severity, not separability).
Default is FOLD discipline is restated.
Three of the four items are folded into this PR.
The single deferral carries the required scope_boundary_crossed citation key.
The auth-resolver refactor (cross-theme) is the deferred item.
Decision is non-terminal; loop continues, iteration counter advances.
Recognizes the recommendations-as-backlog anti-pattern that the new default is built to avoid.
